headline: Datadog raises annual forecast on strong cloud security demand, shares jump 36%
summary: Cloud security firm Datadog on Thursday raised its annual forecast after beating Wall Street expectations ‌for first-quarter results, fueled by high demand for its ‌cloud security products amid rising artificial intelligence adoption. The company's shares were up ​nearly 36% in early trading. The demand for Datadog's monitoring and security tools have been on a rise due to increase in generative AI and cloud migration.

content.summary: Cloud security firm Datadog on Thursday raised its annual forecast after beating Wall Street expectations ‌for first-quarter results, fueled by high demand for its ‌cloud security products amid rising artificial intelligence adoption. The company's shares were up ​nearly 36% in early trading. The demand for Datadog's monitoring and security tools have been on a rise due to increase in generative AI and cloud migration.

headline: Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) Delivers Impressive Q1 CY2026, Stock Jumps 23.8%
summary: Cloud monitoring platform Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 32.2% year on year to $1.01 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($1.08 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 8.3% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.60 per share was 18.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) Delivers Impressive Q1 CY2026, Stock Jumps 23.8%
content.summary: Cloud monitoring platform Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 32.2% year on year to $1.01 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($1.08 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 8.3% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.60 per share was 18.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Datadog Stock Soars After Earnings. Why It’s a Software Bright Spot.
summary: Shares of  Datadog  surged Thursday after the monitoring and analytics platform reported better-than-expected earnings and revenue for the first quarter.  Datadog  posted adjusted earnings of 60 cents a share for the quarter, up from 46 cents last year and ahead of analysts’ consensus estimates of 51 cents.  The company now expects adjusted earnings per share of $2.36 to $2.44 for the full year, up from a previous forecast of $2.08 to $2.16.
